title: Bulk report - Week 12
summary:
Capesize
A more tempered week saw rates soften across all routes with low activity in both basins. Resilience and strong optimism still remains from Owners, yet the tailwinds from smaller classes turned to headwinds early in the week as market sentiment across shipping and commodities took a downward turn. The Capesize rates followed suit through until Thursday before posting a small up-tick.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A more tempered week saw rates soften across all routes with low activity in both basins. Resilience and strong optimism still remains from Owners, yet the tailwinds from smaller classes turned to headwinds early in the week as market sentiment across shipping and commodities took a downward turn.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The Panamax 5 time-charter average yielded a loss of $1,326 on the week to publish on closing at $25,447, which is a realistic reflection of the corrective week the Panamax market has experienced following last week's dramatic rises. Rates in the Atlantic basin eased as demand faltered and pressure slowly built on the nearby position with fewer options for Owners to consider.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: There was a change of direction with rates easing in most areas including Asia. Despite the overall negativity, news of period activity still surfaced with a 63,000-dwt new building being fixed for one year at $20,500.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: At the beginning of the week, the BHSI had its first decline since mid January and the trend continued for the rest of the week. The Atlantic basin largely softened with all routes losing value.
